requirements: The core contributions of the paper 'Shift Parallelism: Low-Latency, High-Throughput LLM Inference for Dynamic Workloads' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: The hardware and software environment matches that of the paper.
vLLM v0.9.2 (or v0.10.1 for reproducibility appendix) has been installed and configured as the base inference engine.

verification.spec: Run `python -c 'import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)'` and verify the version is 0.9.2 or 0.10.1.

sub_tasks.requirements: Baseline frameworks SGLang v0.4.6 and TRT-LLM v0.18.2 have been installed for comparison.

verification.spec: Run `python -c 'import sglang; print(sglang.__version__)'` and verify v0.4.6; verify TRT-LLM via `python -c 'import tensorrt_llm; print(tensorrt_llm.__version__)'` returns 0.18.2.

sub_tasks.requirements: All models used in evaluation have been acquired with FP8 quantization as listed in Table 4.
Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct (FP8-dynamic) is available, with 80 layers, hidden size 8192, 64 Q heads, 8 KV heads.

verification.spec: Load model config via `transformers.AutoConfig.from_pretrained('RedHatAI/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-FP8-dynamic')` and assert num_hidden_layers==80, hidden_size==8192, num_attention_heads==64, num_key_value_heads==8.

sub_tasks.requirements: Qwen3-32B (FP8) is available, with 64 layers, hidden size 5120, 64 Q heads, 8 KV heads.

verification.spec: Load model config via `transformers.AutoConfig.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen3-32B-FP8')` and assert num_hidden_layers==64, hidden_size==5120, num_attention_heads==64, num_key_value_heads==8.

sub_tasks.requirements: Llama-17B-16E (FP8) MoE model is available, with 48 layers, hidden size 5120, 40 Q heads, 8 KV heads, ~109B total / 17B active parameters.

verification.spec: Download the Llama-17B-16E FP8 MoE checkpoint and load its HuggingFace config. Assert num_hidden_layers==48, hidden_size==5120, num_attention_heads==40, num_key_value_heads==8, num_local_experts==16, and that total parameter count is ~109B with ~17B active per token.

sub_tasks.requirements: Qwen3-30B-A3B (FP8) MoE model is available, with 48 layers, hidden size 2048, 32 Q heads, 4 KV heads, ~30B total / 3B active parameters.

verification.spec: Download the Qwen3-30B-A3B FP8 checkpoint and load its HuggingFace config. Assert num_hidden_layers==48, hidden_size==2048, num_attention_heads==32, num_key_value_heads==4, and parameter counts ~30B total / ~3B active per token.

sub_tasks.requirements: All datasets used for benchmarking have been prepared.
Azure LLM Code Trace (15-minute window, 2832 requests) has been downloaded from the AzurePublicDataset (AzureLLMInferenceTrace_code.csv) and prepared for replay.

verification.spec: Download AzureLLMInferenceTrace_code.csv from the AzurePublicDataset repository, filter to a 15-minute window, and verify the resulting trace has ~2832 requests with arrival timestamps, input_tokens, and output_tokens fields populated.

sub_tasks.requirements: Mooncake Conversation Trace (15-minute window, 2727 requests) has been downloaded from the Mooncake GitHub (conversation_trace.jsonl) and prepared for replay.

verification.spec: Download the Mooncake conversation_trace.jsonl from the Mooncake GitHub repository, filter to a 15-minute window, and verify the trace contains ~2727 requests with arrival timestamps, input_length and output_length fields.

sub_tasks.requirements: A bursty synthetic workload has been generated using vLLM's burstiness benchmark, mixing latency- and throughput-critical requests so that four high-traffic bursts are visible in the arrival timeline.

verification.spec: Run the bursty synthetic workload generator (vLLM's burstiness benchmark, modified) that mixes latency- and throughput-critical requests. Plot arrival rate over time and assert that exactly four distinct high-traffic bursts are visible (peaks well above the baseline arrival rate).

sub_tasks.requirements: Synthetic parameterized request datasets with uniform input sizes (2k, 4k, 8k, 16k, 32k, 64k, 128k tokens) and 250 output tokens have been prepared.

verification.spec: Generate synthetic parameterized request datasets where each dataset has fixed input length in {2k,4k,8k,16k,32k,64k,128k} tokens and 250 output tokens. Assert each generated dataset's request token counts match the expected input/output lengths.

sub_tasks.requirements: A mixed dataset combining HumanEval (one-shot) and CodeAct agent traces against SWE-Bench has been prepared for the speculative decoding production evaluation.

verification.spec: Build a mixed dataset combining HumanEval one-shot prompts and CodeAct agent traces from SWE-Bench. Assert the dataset is non-empty and contains both single-shot and multi-turn agentic request types with proper prompt/output format.

sub_tasks.requirements: Performance metrics for measurement have been implemented.
Code measures Time-to-First-Token (TTFT) in milliseconds, defined as the time from prompt submission until first response token.

verification.spec: Mock a request lifecycle with a known prompt submission time and known first-token receive time. Invoke the TTFT measurement function and assert the returned value equals (first_token_time - submit_time) in milliseconds.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code measures Time-Per-Output-Token (TPOT) in milliseconds, defined as the inter-token time after the first response token.

verification.spec: Mock a request streaming N output tokens at known timestamps after the first token. Invoke the TPOT measurement function and assert it returns the mean inter-token interval in ms between successive tokens after the first.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code measures Combined Throughput (tokens/sec) as the total of input plus output tokens processed per unit time.

verification.spec: Feed a known workload trace with total_input_tokens and total_output_tokens processed over a measured duration. Invoke the combined-throughput calculator and assert it returns (total_input_tokens + total_output_tokens) / duration_seconds.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code computes the completion time as TTFT + (# output tokens) × TPOT.

verification.spec: Call the completion-time helper with TTFT=100ms, TPOT=10ms, num_output_tokens=50. Assert the result equals 100 + 50*10 = 600ms. Repeat with another set of values.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code computes Response Speed (#input tokens / TTFT) and Generation Rate (1 / TPOT) per request as plotted in Figure 1.

verification.spec: Call the per-request Response Speed function with num_input_tokens=1000, TTFT=500ms and assert it returns 1000/0.5 = 2000 tok/s. Call the Generation Rate function with TPOT=10ms and assert it returns 1/0.01 = 100 tok/s.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code computes p50, p95, and p99 latency percentiles for TTFT, TPOT, and completion time.

verification.spec: Feed the percentile computation function a list of 1000 known latency values. Assert that returned p50, p95, p99 values match numpy.percentile reference values for TTFT, TPOT, and completion-time columns.

sub_tasks.requirements: Baseline parallelism strategies have been implemented for comparison.
Code implements Tensor Parallelism (TP) using vLLM's built-in TP, partitioning weight matrices row/column-wise across GPUs and using all-reduce communication after attention and MLP outputs.

verification.spec: Launch vLLM serve with --tensor-parallel-size 8 on Llama-70B. Send a single short request and confirm completion. Inspect NCCL traces or model parallel config object to verify that each transformer layer's row-parallel projection issues an all-reduce after attention output and MLP down projections.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements Data Parallelism (DP) using vLLM's built-in DP, running multiple replicas across request boundaries with no intra-request synchronization.

verification.spec: Launch vLLM with --data-parallel-size 8 --tensor-parallel-size 1, send N concurrent requests, verify each replica processes a disjoint subset of requests (no cross-replica all-reduce inside a single request's forward pass) by inspecting NCCL group config / logs.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements head parallelism via column-wise partitioning of the QKV matrix, with the constraint that head parallelism cannot scale beyond the number of attention heads.

verification.spec: Instantiate the QKV linear layer with TP=4 and num_heads=8. Assert the local Q weight matrix is column-partitioned with shape [d, 2*head_dim] (i.e., 2 heads per rank). Also assert that constructing with TP > num_heads raises an error (head parallelism cannot exceed #heads).

sub_tasks.requirements: Ulysses Sequence Parallelism (SP) for inference has been implemented per Section 3.2.
Code partitions the input embedding sequence across SP GPUs at the start of the forward pass (Algorithm 1 Line 1).

verification.spec: In the SP forward pass, instrument the input embedding tensor at layer 0: confirm its sequence-length dimension equals n/SP on each rank (e.g., feed n=64 tokens with SP=4 and assert local shape is [16, d] on each of 4 ranks).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code performs an all-to-all communication on the fused QKV tensor before attention to switch from sequence-partitioned to head-partitioned layout (Algorithm 1 Line 4).

verification.spec: Patch torch.distributed.all_to_all_single to log call sites. Run one SP forward pass with SP>=2; verify exactly one all-to-all is called on the fused QKV tensor immediately after the QKV projection (input shape [n/SP, 3h/TP] -> output shape [n, 3h/(SP*TP)]).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code performs a second all-to-all communication on the attention output to return from head-partitioned to sequence-partitioned layout (Algorithm 1 Line 6).

verification.spec: Hook all_to_all calls during an SP forward pass and confirm a second all-to-all is invoked on the attention output tensor (input shape [n, h/(SP*TP)] -> output shape [n/SP, h/TP]) before the O projection.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code performs an all-gather across SP ranks at the end of the forward pass to assemble the full output embeddings (Algorithm 1 Line 13).

verification.spec: On a 2-GPU process group, run the combined (SP,TP) forward pass with SP=2. Patch torch.distributed.all_gather to record calls. Assert that exactly one all_gather is invoked at the end of the forward pass over the SP group, gathering tensors of shape [n/SP, d] into the full [n, d] output embeddings.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code extends SP to support Grouped Query Attention (GQA), replacing 3*h with h + 2*h_kv in the QKV head count where h_kv is the number of KV heads.

verification.spec: Instantiate SP forward pass on a GQA model (e.g., Llama-70B with h=64 q-heads, h_kv=8). Confirm the fused QKV projection output channel dimension equals (h + 2*h_kv)/TP = (64+16)/TP rather than 3h/TP, and the all-to-all moves QKV with this shape.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements KV cache replication via all-to-all when SP degree exceeds the number of KV heads, replicating KV heads within send buffers so receiving buffers across GPUs hold replicas.

verification.spec: Run SP=8 on Qwen-30B-A3B (h_kv=4 < SP). Inspect the all-to-all send buffer construction: verify that KV head slices are replicated in the send buffer such that after all-to-all the receiving buffers on different ranks contain duplicate KV heads (compare ranks' KV tensors element-wise for replicated heads).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code fuses Q, K, V communications into a single all-to-all collective call rather than separate communications.

verification.spec: Run a forward pass through one transformer layer with SP=2. Patch torch.distributed.all_to_all to record calls. Assert that the pre-attention all-to-all is invoked exactly once on the concatenated QKV tensor (single fused call) rather than three separate calls for Q, K, V.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code pads small batches up to a multiple of the SP degree to evenly distribute tokens across GPUs and avoid load imbalance in low-traffic decoding.

verification.spec: Submit a decode-only batch with batch_size=9 under SP=8. Inspect the actual per-rank token count entering the SP forward pass and assert it is ceil(9/8)*8=16 (i.e., padded to a multiple of SP) with redundant pad tokens marked as such.
verification.paper_cite: Section 3.2.1 Small Batch Size and Load Imbalance

sub_tasks.requirements: Combined (SP, TP) parallelism has been implemented as in Algorithm 1.
Code supports arbitrary (SP, TP) configurations subject to SP × TP = P (total GPUs in the node).

verification.spec: Initialize the parallelism configuration with combinations like (SP=1,TP=8), (SP=2,TP=4), (SP=4,TP=2), (SP=8,TP=1) on an 8-GPU world. Assert each config initializes successfully. Then attempt (SP=3,TP=2) on world_size=8 and assert it raises a validation error because 3*2 != 8.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code performs TP-style all-reduce after the O projection (Algorithm 1 Line 8) and after the MLP down projection (Algorithm 1 Line 11).

verification.spec: Run a forward pass through a transformer layer with TP>1. Patch torch.distributed.all_reduce to count and tag invocations. Assert exactly two TP all_reduces per layer: one immediately after the O projection (line 8) and one after the MLP down projection (line 11), both over the TP process group.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code partitions QKV projection columns by 3h/TP, attention output rows by h/(SP*TP), and MLP up projection columns by d'/TP as specified in Algorithm 1.

verification.spec: Instantiate the layer with n=64, d=512, h=16, d'=2048, SP=2, TP=2. Run a forward pass and assert tensor shapes match Algorithm 1: qkv_heads after projection has 3*h/TP=24 head-dims; after SP all-to-all has h/(SP*TP)=4 heads at full n; attn_o after second all-to-all has shape [n/SP, h/TP]; MLP up activation has shape [n/SP, d'/TP].

sub_tasks.requirements: Shift Parallelism (Section 3.3) has been implemented with dynamic switching between base and shift configurations.
Code defines a base configuration with arbitrary (SP, TP) such that SP × TP = P.

verification.spec: Construct the Shift Parallelism base configuration object with several (SP, TP) pairs whose product equals P (total GPUs). Assert configs (SP=4,TP=2), (SP=2,TP=4), (SP=8,TP=1) on P=8 are all accepted; assert (SP=3,TP=3) on P=8 is rejected because SP*TP != P.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code defines a shift configuration that is always (SP=1, TP=P) spanning the full node.

verification.spec: Inspect the Shift Parallelism config-building code. Construct a shift configuration on a node with P GPUs and assert that the shift config has SP=1 and TP=P (spanning all GPUs in the node). Test for P=2, 4, 8.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements Algorithm 2: at each forward pass, if the number of batched tokens n exceeds the shift parallelism threshold, use the base config; otherwise use the shift config.

verification.spec: Set a known shift threshold T. Issue two forward passes: one with n_tokens > T and one with n_tokens < T. Verify (via logging or by patching Algorithm 1 dispatch) that the first uses the base (SP,TP) config and the second uses the shift (1, SP*TP) config.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code maintains general KV cache invariance: when base config is e.g. (SP=3, TP=2), attention head ordering follows (0,2,4,1,3,5) instead of (0,1,2,3,4,5), and the shift config matches this same ordering to preserve KV cache layout.

verification.spec: Configure base (SP=3, TP=2) on a 6-head test model. After the SP all-to-all in the base forward, dump the global head ordering across ranks and assert it equals (0,2,4,1,3,5). Then run the shift config (TP=6) and verify it shards Q weights in the same (0,2,4,1,3,5) order, producing bitwise-identical KV cache layout to the base config for the same input.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements a general process-to-data mapping that ensures KV cache consistency across arbitrary (SP, TP) base and (SP×TP) shift configurations.

verification.spec: Construct a base config with (SP=3, TP=2) and a shift config with TP=6. Run a forward pass on a small synthetic transformer and assert that the KV cache layout in the shift config matches the head ordering produced by the base config (e.g., (0,2,4,1,3,5) interleaved order rather than natural (0,1,2,3,4,5) order). Verify by comparing KV cache tensors after switching.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the 'separate models' memory strategy: loads two model replicas (base and shift) that share the same KV cache, rather than slicing weights on-the-fly.

verification.spec: Inspect the model loader: verify two distinct nn.Module instances (base_model and shift_model) are instantiated, that both reference the same KVCache object (e.g., `assert base_model.kv_cache is shift_model.kv_cache`), and that linear weights are not aliased (assert weight tensors have separate storage).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code loads shift model weights using a SP_TP group ordered by SP-group rank to ensure the shift model receives the correct shards for KV cache coherency.

verification.spec: Inspect the model loading code for the shift model. Verify that a process group named (or equivalent to) SP_TP is constructed by enumerating ranks in SP-group order (not TP-group order). Assert this group is used for sharding when loading shift-model weights.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code ensures total weight footprint follows (1 + 1/SP) × base-model size, with shift model overhead of 1/SP (e.g. 12.5% when SP=8).

verification.spec: Load the base model with SP=8 configuration, then load the shift model with TP=8 spanning the SP_TP group. Measure the total GPU memory footprint of model weights (excluding KV cache and activations) across all 8 GPUs. Compute the overhead as (total_weights - base_weights) / base_weights * 100. Verify this equals approximately 12.5% (i.e., 1/SP for SP=8). Also test with SP=4 (expected 25%) to confirm the 1/SP formula.

sub_tasks.requirements: Shift Parallelism has been integrated into vLLM via a plug-in system (ArcticInference).
Code uses vLLM's plug-in mechanism to register Shift Parallelism without modifying vLLM core.

verification.spec: Verify the repository registers Shift Parallelism through vLLM's plugin entry-point system (e.g., entry_points in setup.py/pyproject.toml under 'vllm.general_plugins' or similar). Assert no monkey-patching of vLLM internals is required beyond the plugin registration, and that `pip install vllm==0.10.1` works unmodified.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code enables vLLM compilation and CUDA graph capture for both base and shift models separately, with hundreds of captured graphs registered at init and replayed at runtime.

verification.spec: Start a vLLM server with Shift Parallelism enabled and CUDA graph capture on. Inspect logs/captured graph registry and assert that both base and shift models have CUDA graphs captured separately for many batch shapes (>100 graphs). Submit a small request and a larger request and verify both graph sets are exercised.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code is compatible with chunked prefill (default in vLLM) and runs the evaluation experiments with chunked prefill enabled.

verification.spec: Launch vLLM with Shift Parallelism and confirm chunked prefill is enabled (default in vLLM v0.10.1). Submit a long-prompt request and verify that chunked prefill is being used (via logs/metrics showing chunk-by-chunk prefill scheduling). The benchmark scripts in the reproducibility directory must run without disabling chunked prefill.

sub_tasks.requirements: Bursty synthetic workload experiment (Section 4.2.1, Figure 7 and Table 5) on Llama-70B has been executed.
Code runs the bursty workload benchmark on Llama-70B-FP8 with 8xH200 for each of DP, TP, and Shift Parallelism.

verification.spec: Run vLLM's bursty workload benchmark on Llama-3.3-70B-FP8 with 8xH200 separately for DP=8, TP=8, and Shift Parallelism. Verify the benchmark completes and produces TTFT/TPOT/throughput stats for each parallelism.

sub_tasks.requirements: Azure LLM Code Trace replay experiment (Section 4.2.2, Figures 9, 11a) on Llama-70B has been executed.
Code replays the 15-minute Azure LLM Code Trace (2832 requests) on Llama-70B-FP8 / 8xH200 for DP, TP, SP, and Shift Parallelism.

verification.spec: Download the Azure LLM Code Trace (from DOI 10.5281/zenodo.18240909) and replay the first 15 minutes (~2832 requests) on Llama-3.3-70B-FP8 / 8xH200 once each for DP, TP, SP, and Shift Parallelism. Verify each run completes and produces a per-request log of TTFT/TPOT/completion.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code records per-request TTFT, TPOT, and completion time vs. request id for each parallelism (reproducing Figure 9).

verification.spec: Using the provided plot.py script and Azure trace results, produce per-request TTFT/TPOT/completion plots indexed by request id (1..~2832) for all four parallelisms. Assert plots show DP/TP/SP/Shift curves matching qualitative shape of Figure 9 (three prominent bursts visible around request ids ~437, ~1091, ~2181).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code produces CDFs of TTFT, TPOT, and completion time for the Azure trace (reproducing Figure 11a).

verification.spec: Sort the per-request TTFT/TPOT/completion times from the Azure trace and produce CDF plots for DP, TP, SP, Shift Parallel. Assert CDFs qualitatively match Figure 11a (Shift Parallel curve consistently leftmost for completion time).

sub_tasks.requirements: Mooncake Conversation Trace replay experiment (Section 4.2.2, Figures 10, 11b) on Qwen-32B has been executed.
Code replays the 15-minute Mooncake Conversation Trace (2727 requests, ~9 reqs every 3s) on Qwen-32B-FP8 / 8xH200 with FP8 KV cache data type enabled.

verification.spec: Download the Mooncake Conversation Trace and replay the first 15 minutes (~2727 requests, ~9 reqs every 3s) on Qwen3-32B-FP8 / 8xH200, with FP8 KV cache data type enabled (--kv-cache-dtype fp8). Run for DP, TP, SP, Shift Parallel; verify FP8 KV cache flag is honored in vLLM logs.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code records TTFT, TPOT, and completion time per request for DP, TP, SP, and Shift Parallelism (reproducing Figure 10).

verification.spec: Using plot.py, produce per-request TTFT/TPOT/completion plots indexed by request id for the Mooncake trace, separately for DP, TP, SP, Shift Parallelism. Assert plots qualitatively match Figure 10 (DP/TP TTFT growing indefinitely, SP/Shift remaining bounded).

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduce the conclusion that DP and TP cannot keep up with traffic (TTFT grows indefinitely) while SP and Shift Parallelism sustain finite completion times.

verification.spec: Simulate or replay a high-traffic conversation workload (similar to Mooncake trace) sending ~9 requests every 3 seconds with medium input/long output to Qwen-32B-FP8 on a single 8xH200 node. Run DP, TP, SP, and Shift Parallelism configurations for 15 minutes each. For each configuration, fit a linear regression to TTFT vs request_id (in milliseconds per request). Verify that DP and TP show a positive growing slope (TTFT increases unboundedly, slope > some threshold), while SP and Shift Parallelism show a near-flat slope (slope_SP / slope_TP < ~0.1, indicating sustained finite completion times). Report the ratio of slopes.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code produces CDFs of TTFT, TPOT, and completion time for the Mooncake trace (reproducing Figure 11b).

verification.spec: Sort per-request TTFT/TPOT/completion times from the Mooncake trace and produce CDF plots for DP, TP, SP, Shift Parallel. Assert CDFs qualitatively match Figure 11b (Shift Parallel CDF reaches 100% at finite time while DP/TP do not).

sub_tasks.requirements: Context-size sweep benchmark (Section 4.3.2, Figure 13) has been executed.
Reproduce the claim that Shift Parallelism provides up to 6.97× faster response than DP and 1.56× faster response than TP across input context sizes.

verification.spec: Sweep input sequence lengths 2k..128k (250 output tokens) on Llama-70B-FP8 with DP, TP, and Shift Parallelism in low-traffic (one request at a time). Compute response_speed = input_tokens/TTFT for each. Find the maximum ratio of Shift_response_speed to DP_response_speed across context sizes and verify it reaches ~6.97x; also check max ratio vs TP reaches ~1.56x.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduce the claim that Shift Parallelism provides up to 2.45× faster generation than DP and similar TPOT to TP across context sizes.

verification.spec: From the input-length sweep results, compute TPOT_DP / TPOT_Shift across all (model, length) pairs and find the max ratio. Assert max ratio is up to ~2.45x. Also compute TPOT_Shift / TPOT_TP and assert it is in [0.9, 1.2] across the sweep (similar to TP).

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduce the claim that Shift Parallelism obtains up to 1.51× higher peak throughput than TP.

verification.spec: Run vLLM benchmark with Llama-70B FP8 on 8xH200 at saturating concurrency (batch of thousands of requests, 4k input / 250 output tokens). Measure peak combined throughput (tok/s) for TP=8 baseline and for Shift Parallelism (base SP=8, shift TP=8). Compute ratio Shift_throughput / TP_throughput and verify it is approximately 1.51x.

sub_tasks.requirements: Latency vs. arrival rate experiment (Section 4.3.3, Figure 14) has been executed.
Code varies request arrival rate from low to high (sweeping across req/s on a log scale) using 8k input / 250 output tokens for DP, TP, and Shift Parallelism on Llama-70B and Qwen-32B.

verification.spec: Run the arrival-rate sweep benchmark for DP, TP, and Shift Parallelism using 8k input / 250 output tokens on both Llama-70B-FP8 and Qwen-32B-FP8, sweeping arrival rates across a log scale (e.g., 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 req/s). Verify the script produces per-(parallelism, model, rate) result files with TTFT and TPOT.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code measures and plots completion time (ms) vs. arrival rate (req/s).

verification.spec: Verify that a plotting routine ingests the arrival-rate sweep results and emits a figure with arrival rate (req/s, log scale) on the x-axis and completion time (ms) on the y-axis, with separate curves per parallelism scheme. Inspect the output figure file exists and contains the expected axes/labels.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduce the conclusion that TP and DP curves cross over at some critical arrival rate, while Shift Parallelism is strictly lower than both across the entire arrival-rate sweep.

verification.spec: Using the arrival-rate sweep data, verify that the DP curve completion time exceeds TP at the lowest arrival rate, TP exceeds DP at the highest rate (crossover exists), and Shift Parallelism's completion time is strictly less than min(DP, TP) at every measured arrival rate (within 5% tolerance) for both Llama-70B and Qwen-32B.

sub_tasks.requirements: Cost breakdown analysis (Section 4.4, Figure 15) has been executed.
Code instruments per-component timing of vLLM engine overhead, model compute, all-reduce, attention, and all-to-all for each parallelism config.

verification.spec: Inspect the codebase for instrumentation that times the following components per batch: vLLM engine overhead (computed by subtracting forward pass from total), model compute (matmuls), all-reduce collectives, attention kernel, and all-to-all collectives. Confirm timing hooks exist and produce per-component durations in the output log/CSV.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code produces the breakdown for Llama-70B at input lengths 2K, 8K, 32K, 64K, 128K under 4x(TP=2), TP=8, and (TP=2, SP=4) configurations.

verification.spec: Run the cost-breakdown benchmark on Llama-70B-FP8 at input lengths {2K, 8K, 32K, 64K, 128K} under three configurations: 4x(TP=2) DP, full TP=8, and (TP=2, SP=4). Verify result file contains per-component durations (vLLM, model, all-reduce, attention, all-to-all) for each (config, seqlen) combination.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code produces the breakdown for Qwen-32B at input lengths 2K, 8K, 32K, 64K, 128K under DP=8, TP=8, SP=8 configurations.

verification.spec: Run the cost-breakdown benchmark on Qwen-32B-FP8 at input lengths {2K, 8K, 32K, 64K, 128K} under DP=8, TP=8, and SP=8 configurations. Verify per-component timings appear in the result file for each (config, seqlen) pair.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduce the conclusions that (1) SP has lower communication cost than TP and (2) attention time and vLLM engine overhead dominate at long sequences and small models respectively.

verification.spec: From the cost-breakdown results, verify that (1) the all-to-all + collectives time for SP/Shift configs is less than the all-reduce time for TP=8 on each model at each sequence length, and (2) attention time as fraction of total batch time monotonically grows with sequence length (e.g., >40% at 128K), while vLLM overhead fraction dominates at 2K on Qwen-32B (smaller model).

sub_tasks.requirements: Production-environment integration experiment (Section 4.5, Figure 16) has been executed.

Code compares the combined production stack against vLLM, SGLang, and TRT-LLM in both latency-opt (TP) and throughput-opt (DP) configurations, with their best-available speculative decoding enabled, on a real Llama-70B dataset.

verification.spec: Run benchmark comparing the production stack (vLLM + Shift Parallelism + SwiftKV + spec decoding) against vLLM, SGLang, and TRT-LLM each in latency-opt (TP) and throughput-opt (DP) configurations with their best available speculative decoding enabled. sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation studies from the paper

Code has been implemented and run such that both weight-handling strategies for Shift Parallelism are implemented: (i) on-the-fly slicing of base-model weights at the linear layer, and (ii) loading separate base and shift models that share the KV cache. The total weight memory footprint of strategy (ii) is measured and confirmed to incur a 1/SP memory overhead (e.g., ~12.5% extra weights at SP=8), while strategy (i) incurs zero replication overhead but is shown to be slower due to FP8 matrix transposition on Hopper tensor cores.

verification.spec: Instantiate Llama-70B-FP8 under Shift Parallelism with SP=8 using strategy (ii); measure GPU weight memory and confirm overhead = 1/SP = 12.5% +/- tolerance vs. strategy (i). Also benchmark a single forward pass under both strategies and confirm strategy (ii) is faster than strategy (i) in TPOT.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the end-to-end cost-breakdown is decomposed into vLLM overhead, model compute, all-reduce, attention, and all-to-all components across input sequence lengths {2K, 8K, 32K, 64K, 128K} for Llama-70B with (TP=2, SP=4) base config. The attention time should grow super-linearly with sequence length and become the dominant cost at 128K input, matching Figure 15(a).

verification.spec: Instrument Shift Parallelism forward pass with CUDA event timers for each component; run batch of requests at input lengths 2K..128K; assert attention fraction of total time at 128K >= 50% and all-to-all is smaller than all-reduce in TP-only config, matching Figure 15(a) trend.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: minimum fraction of total time spent in attention at 128K input

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the Mooncake Conversation Trace is replayed on Qwen-32B-FP8 first with FP16 KV cache dtype and then with FP8 KV cache dtype on a single 8xH200 node. The FP16 configuration should exhibit unbounded queueing/wait-time growth (KV cache overflow); the FP8 configuration should reduce queue growth for DP/TP but still fail to sustain the trace, while Shift Parallelism can sustain the trace with FP8 KV cache.

verification.spec: Replay Mooncake Conversation Trace (15 min) on Qwen-32B-FP8 under (kv_cache_dtype=fp16) and (kv_cache_dtype=fp8); for each, record max TTFT growth over time under DP=8 and Shift Parallelism. Assert FP16 DP/TP TTFT grows >10x by trace end, FP8 Shift Parallelism remains bounded (max TTFT < 30s).

sub_tasks.paper_unit: ms (Shift Parallel max TTFT upper bound on Mooncake)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that request completion time is measured for DP=8, TP=8, and Shift Parallelism on Llama-70B-FP8 across a sweep of arrival rates (e.g., 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 req/s) with 8K input / 250 output tokens. The DP and TP curves should cross at an intermediate arrival rate (TP wins at low rate, DP wins at high rate), while Shift Parallelism's completion time is at or below the minimum of DP and TP across all arrival rates, matching Figure 14(a).

verification.spec: Sweep arrival rate {0.5,1,2,4,8,16} req/s with Poisson arrivals, 8K/250 token requests on Llama-70B-FP8; record median completion time for DP, TP, Shift. Verify TP < DP at <=1 req/s, DP < TP at >=8 req/s, and Shift <= min(TP,DP) at every rate within tolerance. Report Shift Parallelism completion time at 1 req/s.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: ms (Shift Parallel completion time at ~1 req/s, Llama-70B)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that a compounding-effect ablation on Llama-70B is performed by progressively enabling optimizations: baseline vLLM TP (latency opt.), +SwiftKV, +Speculative Decoding, +Shift Parallelism. Each successive addition should reduce P95 completion latency and/or increase combined throughput, with the final stack achieving the lowest P95 latency AND highest throughput simultaneously, as in Figure 16.

verification.spec: Run real-world-trace benchmark on Llama-70B-FP8 with 4 configurations: (a) TP baseline, (b) +SwiftKV, (c) +SwiftKV+SpecDec, (d) +SwiftKV+SpecDec+ShiftParallel. Record P95 completion latency and combined throughput for each. Assert monotonic improvement in at least one metric per step and final (d) Pareto-dominates (a). Report final throughput.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: tokens/sec combined throughput with full stack on Llama-70B

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Shift Parallelism is benchmarked on both a dense model (Llama-70B or Qwen-32B) and a sparse MoE model (Qwen3-30B-A3B with 4 KV heads requiring KV cache replication, or Llama-4-17B-16E with (SP=4,TP=2) base config). The MoE models should achieve >=2x higher peak combined throughput than the dense Llama-70B at 8K input, validating Section 4.6's claim that sparse models attain higher throughput and lower latency due to fewer active parameters.

verification.spec: Benchmark peak throughput at 8K input / 250 output on (i) Llama-70B-FP8 and (ii) Qwen3-30B-A3B-FP8 under Shift Parallelism on 8xH200. Assert Q30B-A3B throughput / L70B throughput >= 2.0 within tolerance.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: x throughput ratio (Q30B-A3B vs L70B at 8K input)

verification.spec: Query GPU properties via torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0) for total_memory (~141 GB) and run a bandwidth microbenchmark (e.g., large device-to-device copy or NCCL all-reduce of large buffer / cudaMemcpy benchmark) to measure HBM bandwidth. sub_tasks.paper_unit: TB/s HBM bandwidth (memory capacity 141 GB also verified)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the H200 GPU is verified to deliver approximately 1979 TFLOPS peak dense FP8 matrix multiplication throughput on tensor cores.

verification.spec: Run a large square FP8 GEMM (e.g., M=N=K=16384) using cuBLAS / cublasLt or torch._scaled_mm with FP8 inputs on a single H200; measure achieved TFLOPS = 2*M*N*K / time_sec / 1e12 averaged over many iterations. Assert measured TFLOPS within 20% of 1979.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the intra-node NVSwitch bandwidth on the 8xH200 instance is measured at approximately 900 GB/s per GPU.

verification.spec: Run an NCCL all-reduce or send/recv bandwidth benchmark (e.g., nccl-tests all_reduce_perf) with large message sizes across all 8 H200 GPUs; convert busBW to per-GPU NVLink bandwidth. Assert measured per-GPU NVLink bandwidth within 20% of 900 GB/s.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that experiments execute on an AWS p5en.48xlarge instance (or equivalent) with 8x NVIDIA H200 GPUs, each providing 141 GB HBM and 4.8 TB/s memory bandwidth. verification.spec: Run nccl-tests all_reduce_perf -b 1G -e 1G -g 8; record busbw; assert busbw >= 700 GB/s.

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code has been implemented such that the Ulysses SP partition function correctly identifies a 50% efficiency under load imbalance when batch size = 9 and SP = 8 (i.e., one rank handles 2 tokens while 7 ranks handle 1 token each).

verification.spec: Call the SP token-distribution / padding utility with batch_size=9, sp_degree=8. Without padding, assert max_tokens_per_rank=2, min_tokens_per_rank=1, and efficiency = mean/max = (9/8)/2 = 0.5625 (i.e., parallel efficiency relative to balanced 2-token-per-rank load = 9/(8*2) = 0.5625, paper rounds to ~50%). With padding, assert padded length = 16 (next multiple of 8 >= 9 satisfying 'even per-rank load of 2').
verification.paper_cite: Section 3.2.1, small batch / load imbalance example

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the additional GPU memory footprint of the Shift Parallelism 'separate models' weight replication is 12.5% of the base model weight footprint when SP=8.

verification.spec: Initialize Shift Parallelism with base config (SP=8, TP=1) and the corresponding shift config (TP=8) on a representative model (e.g., Llama-70B-FP8) on 8xH200. Measure per-GPU GPU memory used by (a) base model weights only and (b) base+shift model weights. Compute overhead = (b - a) / a. Assert |overhead - 0.125| / 0.125 <= 0.20.
verification.paper_cite: Section 3.3.2, memory overhead formula 1/SP, example SP=8 -> 12.5%

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Shift Parallelism shows ~12.5% memory overhead from the shift-model weight replication when SP=8 (i.e., 1/SP overhead)

verification.spec: Instantiate the Shift Parallelism configuration with SP=8, TP=1 on a model; query GPU memory used by base-model weights vs. base+shift-model weights; compute overhead = (shift_model_bytes)/(base_model_bytes); assert within tolerance of 0.125

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-FP8-dynamic under Shift Parallelism on 8xH200 achieves TTFT, TPOT, and peak combined throughput matching Figure 13(a) within tolerance at input lengths {2K, 8K, 32K, 128K} with 250 output tokens.
